title: Cinnabarinea oligotricha
intro: Cinnabarinea oligotricha, commonly known as C. oligotricha, is a plant species belonging to the family Asparagaceae, native to Australia. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which may aid in reducing oxidative stress and alleviating symptoms of various health conditions. Bioactive constituents of C. oligotricha include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to make herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and capsules, although further research is needed to fully understand its efficacy and potential interactions with other medications.
taxonomy: Cinnabarinea oligotricha is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asphodelaceae. It belongs to the genus Cinnabarinea, which is a group of plants native to Australia. This genus is further classified within the subfamily Hemerocallidoideae. The family Asphodelaceae is a large group of plants that includes many species of succulents and flowering plants. Cinnabarinea oligotricha is a unique and endemic species found in the Northern Territory of Australia.
